Case Study

Zero Grappler

Minimal no_std async sensor-to-inference pipelines for embedded Rust.

Prototype crate Rust Embedded no_std Embassy TinyML
Back to workbench ← Mosaico Nephtys →

Why it exists

Embedded ML examples often entangle hardware access, feature extraction, and inference logic, which makes reuse harder than it should be.

Technical center

The crate splits the pipeline into three traits, keeps buffer sizes at compile time, routes stages through Embassy channels, and avoids heap allocation entirely.

What to show next

One public board demo and a concrete sensor-plus-inference example would make the design feel finished.

Repository Related article
Hardware demo

A future sensor-to-inference trace from the Raspberry hardware target once the first public demo lands.

Pipeline notes

A photo or short note from the embedded setup once the hardware loop is running end to end.
